label: plain_text
text:
Now, let us see what happens to the trigonometric ratios of Z A, when it is made
larger and larger in △ABC till it becomes 90°. As Z A gets larger and larger, Z C gets
smaller and smaller. Therefore, as in the case above, the length of the side AB
goeson
decreasing. The point A gets closer to point B. Finally when Z A is very close to 90°,
 C becomes very close to 0° a
and the side AC almost coincides with side BC
(see Fig. 8.18).
